content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN strings. Your task is to group the strings that are anagrams of each other.
Complete the given function.

**Note**: An anagram is a string formed by rearranging the letters of a another string, using all the original letters exactly once. 

For example, **"listen"** and **"silent"** are anagrams of each other because they contain the same letters.


Input Format

The first line of input contains an integer NNN — the number of strings.
The next NNN lines contain strings.


Output Format

Complete the function which return the vector of string vectors. each string vector are group of anagram strings.
You can return the groups of anagrams in any order. You will get a text **Nicely Grouped!** if grouping is done correctly, otherwise **WA**.
constraints: All strings are of lowercase latin letters.
Size of each string is at most 10
content.inputFormat: First line contains N, the number of strings. Each of the next N lines contains a string.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, return a vector of string vectors where each string vector is a group of anagram strings.

solution.approach: The key insight is to use a hash map where the sorted version of each string is the key and the value is a list of strings that are anagrams of the key. This approach works because anagrams will have the same characters when sorted. The time complexity is O(NMlogM) where N is the number of strings and M is the maximum length of a string, due to the sorting operation. The space complexity is O(NM) for storing the strings in the hash map.
solution.timeComplexity: O(NMlogM) per test case, O(TNMlogM) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(NM) — the dominant structure is the hash map.
